name: End of Term Archive
description: A coordinated effort to capture and preserve U.S. government websites and datasets during presidential transitions. Operating since 2008, it ensures continuity of access to public digital information, including data tools and web-based resources that may otherwise be altered or removed.

name: Environmental Data & Governance Initiative (EDGI)
description: A research and advocacy collaborative focused on environmental data justice, governance, and accountability. EDGI monitors changes to federal data systems, develops civic tools (such as web monitoring trackers), and contributes to preservation efforts through partnerships like the Public Environmental Data Partners.

name: ICPSR
description: ICPSR is a long-standing central repository for social and behavioral science data; DataLumos is an ICPSR archive for valuable public U.S. government data resources, emphasizing safekeeping and dissemination.

name: IPUMS
description: A long-standing data integration and dissemination project that harmonizes global census and survey data across time and geography. It emphasizes accessibility, documentation, and comparability, providing a critical access layer for government-produced demographic and socioeconomic data.

name: Dryad
description: A generalist repository available to help with data publication, storage, and preservation.

name: Webrecorder
description: Develops open-source tools for high-fidelity web archiving, enabling users to capture and preserve interactive websites as they function in real time. It serves as key technical infrastructure for preserving web-based data and digital experiences.

name: Wayback Machine
description: An initiative of the Internet Archive building a digital library of Internet sites and other cultural artifacts in digital form.

name: America's Essential Data
description: Collaborative effort dedicated to documenting the value that data produced by the federal government provides for American lives.

name: Policy Commons Open Collection
description: Curates, preserves, and provides access to policy-relevant “grey literature,” including reports, briefs, and multimedia content. It focuses on safeguarding and organizing materials at risk of disappearance, complementing dataset-focused preservation efforts.

name: Data Liberation Project
description: Run by BigLocalNews and MuckRock, focused on liberating government data.

name: Silencing Science Tracker
description: Tracks government attempts to restrict scientific research, education, or discussion.

name: The LGBTQ+ Archive
description: Independent effort to preserve and make accessible information on health, legal rights, and safety for the LGBTQ+ community.

name: American Geophysical Union (AGU)
description: Highlights and elevates “impactful datasets” that support scientific research and decision-making, including through community nominations, expert commentary, and special journal collections. AGU also convenes stakeholders and funds initiatives focused on strengthening data resilience and stewardship across the Earth and space sciences.

name: Climate-Ocean Data Action Network (CDAN)
description: An informal, expert-driven coalition advancing open guidance for safeguarding ocean and climate data. CDAN convenes practitioners to share best practices, identify at-risk assets, and develop frameworks for preservation, prioritization, and future ocean-climate information system design.

name: Data Foundation - Climate Data Collaborative
description: Serves as connective infrastructure for U.S. climate and environmental data ecosystems, coordinating stakeholders to improve measurement, monitoring, reporting, and verification (MMRV) systems. It reduces duplication and accelerates the “data-to-action” pipeline, including through initiatives like the GHG Coalition.

name: Group on Reference Quality Data sets (GRQDs)
description: Focuses on identifying, prioritizing, and preserving “reference-quality” datasets that underpin climate science and key downstream products. The group develops strategies to safeguard high-value, long-term observational records at risk of degradation or loss.

name: Keeling Curve Foundation (KCF)
description: Advances the continuity of critical Earth system observations by identifying priority datasets and connecting them with funding pathways. Through its Data Continuity Initiative, KCF acts as a bridge between scientists and funders to sustain long-term measurement records.

name: Cornerstone Data Initiative
description: Develops and maintains foundational datasets for corporate carbon accounting, including the integration of USEEIO and CEDA into a unified open-source multi-regional input-output (MRIO) model. The initiative provides globally relevant emissions factors to support Scope 3 accounting and decision-making.

name: FracTracker (Federal Accountability)
description: Empowers communities with data, mapping tools, and analysis to investigate and document the impacts of oil and gas development. It promotes transparency and environmental accountability by making complex datasets accessible and actionable at local and regional levels.

name: Environmental & Health Data & Analysis Trust
description: Builds a modern, interoperable data and analytics platform to support environmental and public health decision-making. Designed for federal, state, Tribal, and local use, it integrates diverse datasets into a consistent, open architecture with tools tailored to real-world policy and planning needs.

name: New York Climate Exchange (NYCE)
description:
Launched Climate Data Stewardship initiative to preserve and activate federal climate data.

Preserving/stewarding/activating federal climate & environmental data; building frameworks for open data governance, access, and usability.

name: Public Environmental Data Partners (PEDP)
description: A coalition of organizations working to preserve, protect, and expand access to environmental and climate data. PEDP coordinates across partners on data rescue, tool development, outreach, and advocacy to strengthen the broader data ecosystem.

name: The Data Rescue Project
description: A centralized clearinghouse for at-risk U.S. government data, coordinating efforts to identify, rescue, clean, and catalog datasets. It provides a searchable portal and tracking tools to support distributed preservation and access efforts.

name: Climate.us
description: A public-facing platform and successor to Climate.gov, focused on translating climate science and data into accessible, actionable information. It aims to sustain and expand public climate literacy by delivering authoritative data products, tools, and explainers for broad audiences.

name: CODE (Center for Open Data Enterprise)
description: A nonprofit that advances open data policy and practice through cross-sector research, convenings, and partnerships. CODE supports efforts to improve environmental data access, governance, and interoperability, helping align public and private stakeholders around shared data infrastructure goals.

name: The Impact Project
description: Builds data platforms and analytical tools to demonstrate the value of public programs and services. It aggregates cross-sector data and supports partnerships aimed at protecting data infrastructure and enabling evidence-based decision-making.

name: Harvard's Library Innovation Lab Team
description: Develops tools and infrastructure to preserve and verify public U.S. government data. Its “Government Data Vault” initiative enables downloading, cryptographic verification, and sharing of datasets (including Data.gov holdings) to ensure long-term integrity and access.

name: National Academies (NASEM)
description: Convenes cross-sector experts to explore strategies for improving coordination, governance, and resilience of Earth observation systems. The workshop series helps shape shared frameworks for sustaining data, infrastructure, and collaboration across agencies and disciplines.

name: New America
description: U.S. think tank and civic enterprise that conducts research and policy work on issues including technology, data governance, and smart cities.

name: Open Environmental Data Project (OEDP)
description: Nonprofit that builds spaces, tools, and governance models to improve access to and use of environmental data for participatory environmental decision-making.

name: PANGAEA
description: Joint facility of the Alfred Wegener Institute and MARUM that serves as an open-access data library and publisher for georeferenced Earth and environmental science data, providing long-term archiving and publication.

name: Source Cooperative
description: Nonprofit data publishing utility that lets trusted organizations upload, share, and access data at scale using commodity cloud object storage and standard URLs

name: Stanford Data Commons
description: Platform providing access to Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability research data and thousands of datasets in Google Data Commons. Open-source hosting that aggregates data spatially and temporally.

name: Stanford WaterShed
description: Initiative in partnership with Stanford Sustainable Solutions Lab. 'Cornerstone' hub for open access to US USEIO model, EPA carbon accounting model, and Watershed's global carbon accounting model (CEDA).

name: Urban Institute
description: Washington, D.C. based private, nonprofit research organization conducting independent economic and social policy research and providing data tools such as the Education Data Portal and Explorer.

name: UW Climate Risk Lab
description: Multidisciplinary research and innovation center at University of Washington's Foster School of Business that develops climate-risk data, analysis, and tools to support climate-related financial risk decisions.

name: AGCI's SHIP initiative
description: Field-building initiative designed to safeguard and modernize climate capacity for scaled global action, with a durable network of climate experts embedded across data, science, policy, implementation, and frontline communities.

name: AWS
description: Private-sector cloud provider used to host and distribute government and public-interest datasets.

name: Climate Central
description: Nonprofit climate science and communications organization that hosts and presents the Billion Dollar Disaster Dataset.

name: Climate READi
description: Electric Power Research Institute (EPRI) initiative (REsilience and ADaptation initiative) that convenes utilities, regulators, and national labs to develop a comprehensive framework for assessing physical climate risk to power systems and guiding resilience investments.

name: Data Curation Network
description: Membership organization of institutional and non-profit data repositories that shares curation staff and best practices to make research data more ethical, reusable, and FAIR.

name: Earth Science Information Partners (ESIP)
description: Community and 501(c)(3) nonprofit that brings together Earth science data professionals and organizations to improve interoperability, access, and use of Earth science data.

name: Environmental Policy Innovation Center (EPIC)
description: Builds policies delivering improvements in speed of environmental progress. Four focus areas: Agriculture, Restoration, Technology, Water. Key partner of PEDP. Significant expertise on environmental justice tools. Experience working with state governments to increase data capacity. Founded 2017.

name: ESRI
description: Private GIS software company best known for the ArcGIS platform, widely used for mapping, spatial analytics, and climate- and environmental-related decision-support.

name: Google
description: Private-sector technology company and cloud provider that hosts public-sector and government data infrastructure.

name: Kaggle
description: Online platform for data science competitions, datasets, and notebooks that hosts a large community-curated repository of datasets.

name: Reinsurance Industry Association
description: Industry association that publishes aggregate reinsurance market analytics, underwriting results, annual statement analysis, and historical loss-development studies. reinsurance

name: Archive Today
description: Privately funded, on-demand web archiving service that saves snapshots of web pages (including JavaScript-heavy sites) as static copies.

name: Civil Service Strong
description: Coalition initiative providing resources and support to defend, support, and rebuild a nonpartisan professional U.S. civil service.

name: Climate Mirror / MIT Climate Lab
description: Climate Mirror is a volunteer effort to mirror and back up U.S. federal climate datasets and websites, storing redundant copies in many locations worldwide; the MIT Climate Lab reference relates to separate MIT projects that visualize climate risk but is not the operator of Climate Mirror.

name: Climate Program Portal
description: Online resource that tracks and synthesizes climate-related investments and funding opportunities under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and the Inflation Reduction Act, including dashboards and archives for program information.

name: Energy Information Administration
description: U.S. Department of Energy statistical agency that collects, analyzes, and disseminates official energy data and forecasts for policy, markets, and the public.

name: Environmental Protection Agency Enforcement Database Archive
description: EPA's enforcement and compliance data are made available through Enforcement and Compliance History Online (ECHO) and related online data sources, which provide historical enforcement, violations, and case data.

name: European Data Portal
description: Central portal providing access to open data from EU institutions and European national, regional, and local portals, operated by the Publications Office of the European Union.

name: Federal Data Forum
description: Online hub and community that connects federal data users to share information about threats to U.S. federal statistics, coordinate advocacy, and access resources on federal data products.

name: Government Accountability Office Reports Archive
description: Collections that provide long-term access to GAO reports, testimonies, and Comptroller General decisions on federal programs and finances.

name: ICPSR / DataLumos
description: An international consortium supporting data access, curation, and analysis in the social sciences. It enables community contributions of datasets, enhances discoverability through metadata, and provides preservation services, though capacity for very large datasets may be limited.

name: Massive Data Institute (Georgetown)
description: Interdisciplinary research center at Georgetown's McCourt School of Public Policy that uses modern computing and large-scale data (administrative, organic, survey, etc.) to answer public policy questions.

name: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
description: U.S. federal science agency that monitors weather, oceans, atmosphere, and climate and manages marine resources, providing extensive environmental and climate data.

name: Open Energy Information
description: U.S. Department of Energy supported open platform providing energy data, tools, and analyses for policymakers, researchers, and market participants.

name: Open Government Canada
description: Government of Canada's open government and open data portal, providing access to federal datasets and information.

name: Preservation of Electronic Government Information
description: Initiative to address national concerns about preserving at-risk electronic U.S. government information for long-term public use.

name: ProPublica
description: Investigative journalism nonprofit that also operates the ProPublica Data Store and APIs to share cleaned and structured datasets used in reporting.

name: Public Environmental Data Project
description: Volunteer coalition committed to preserving and providing public access to federal environmental data and screening tools.

name: Publications Office of the European Union.
description: Official EU interinstitutional office that publishes and provides access to EU law, publications, and data, including operating the EU's open data portal.

name: Safeguarding Research and Culture
description: Volunteer-driven initiative creating alternative, decentralized infrastructure to archive and disseminate cultural heritage and scientific knowledge, focusing on public data at risk of deletion or alteration.

name: Springshare
description: Ed-tech company providing library-focused, web-based applications (e.g., LibGuides) delivered as software-as-a-service.

name: Stanford's Big Local News
description: A data journalism and infrastructure initiative that aggregates, standardizes, and publishes datasets—particularly in sustainability and local reporting contexts. It emphasizes access, collaboration, and integration (e.g., with Data Commons) rather than primary preservation.

name: The Center for Open Data Enterprise
description: 501(c)(3) nonprofit that works with governments and organizations to use open and shared data for the public good, hosting roundtables and producing reports.

name: The World Bank
description: International financial institution that also provides extensive open access to development data through its Data Catalog and APIs.

name: United States Geological Survey
description: U.S. federal science agency that studies landscapes, natural resources, and natural hazards and provides extensive geospatial and scientific data.

name: USGEO
description: Federal interagency mechanism that coordinates U.S. civil Earth observation activities, improves Earth system data management and interoperability, and identifies high-priority uses for Earth observation data.
